rows: Avalon Hotel and Bungalows Palm Springs

Very expensive and not worth it. The bar was fine but not free, and we were charged an extra $35 per night on top of the room rate (and this was separate from parking). This fee was never disclosed, and when I called, they said it covered water and other amenities—yet we didn’t even have water in the room.The room also had a strong odor, and the window was open when we arrived and we kept it open and run the A/C to manage it. We left one night early. I do not recommend this hotel.

The air-conditioning unit was located inside the room and was quite noisy. The room felt dated and had an unpleasant odor; keeping the windows slightly open helped make it more tolerable. Parking was available about two blocks away.

This review is for the afternoon tea at the restaurant downstairs.I love an afternoon tea and have been to many across the city and New York. we came for my birthday and left extremely disappointed by the quality, especially for the price (over $200 for 2 with tip). That burning feeling you get when you were charged a luxury price for a poor experience? Yeah, if you’d like to avoid that don’t get tea here!The main issues: the scones were flat, like they had been melted before baking to a crisp. They were served with whipped cream, not clotted cream. The sweets were clearly thawed from frozen (purchased from a commissary like Sysco I imagine) and tasted like sawdust. The tea sandwiches were dry and congealed like they’d been sitting out for hours. And they used tea bags instead of the loose leaf tea you’d expect from a nice hotel.No blame to the wait staff, who were great. This was entirely about the quality of the food and tea which frankly shocked us for the price they charge.
$140 for a slice of beef Wellington and stale cookies. Gordon Ramsey gives you the whole wellington for less money. Don’t waste your time here.

No microwave in my room. When I asked to heat up my food downstairs I was met with attitude. When I got my bill back at the end of my stay I was billed with so many fees including the "complimentary " breakfast

It's nicely decorated but many issues.I stayed here for a week- that whole week the hotel WiFi wasn't working.There was no microwave in my room.  I went to heat my food up in the kitchen and the staff gave me issues.When I got the breakdown of the bill there were so many hidden fees and charges including the "complimentary breakfast " ( pastries and coffee).
Nice but overpriced. It was much better before Covid when it was a French restaurant

Great stay overshadowed by poor service and missing propertyI really wanted to love this hotel — and for the most part, I did. The rooms were beautiful, the property well-kept, and the atmosphere relaxing. I had multiple meals on site and initially thought I’d found my new favorite getaway spot.But two things completely ruined the experience for me.First, the bar experience was embarrassingly stingy. My friend ordered a St. Germain spritzer and, when she politely mentioned she couldn’t taste the liqueur, the server said adding a splash more would require charging her for a full extra shot. We’re not talking about doubling the drink — just enough to balance the flavor. At a property that markets itself as upscale, that kind of nickel-and-diming over an ounce of elderflower is tacky and tone-deaf.Second, and far worse: my charger went missing after checkout. I called within a few hours of leaving — before the next guest had even checked in — and explained exactly where I’d left it plugged in. The front desk claimed it wasn’t there. How is that possible? I know precisely where I left it. The only people who had access were housekeeping. If an item disappears in that short window, that’s not a coincidence — that’s a theft problem.It’s disappointing when a property that gets the big things right completely fails on integrity and basic hospitality. Until management addresses staff accountability and customer care, I can’t recommend this hotel — no matter how charming it looks on the surface.

It has a cool vintage vibe and a really cozy feel right in the heart of Culver City. The rooms are comfortable, the bar is fun, and everything you need is just a short walk away.

I was not staying there, but restaurant is really good. Tasty onion soup and good wine. Top 10000 there bar/restaurant atmosphere, amazing!!!

No microwave in my room. When I asked to heat up my food downstairs I was met with attitude. When I got my bill back at the end of my stay I was billed with so many fees including the "complimentary " breakfast

I loved my stay at The Culver Hotel. It is located in a beautiful area in the heart of Culver City. The team was engaging and created a very elevated experience. The room was beautiful, the bed was extremely comfortable, and everything was spotless. I also really appreciated the fresh fruit and welcome note upon our arrival. I had dinner at the restaurant, which felt elevated, and the server was approachable, helpful, and attentive. We will be back - I would highly recommend to stay here.

The Culver hotel is a quaint boutique hotel nestled in the heart of Culver City. The rooms are spacious and well decorated. The Restaurant offered live music, great food and delicious drinks and dessert. It is easily walkable to literally everything!

It's nicely decorated but many issues.I stayed here for a week- that whole week the hotel WiFi wasn't working.There was no microwave in my room.  I went to heat my food up in the kitchen and the staff gave me issues.When I got the breakdown of the bill there were so many hidden fees and charges including the "complimentary breakfast " ( pastries and coffee).

We hosted a client dinner at the Culver Hotel, and it was amazing! Their staff is incredibly helpful, and the space is beautiful. Our server, Luca, was fantastic as well. Highly recommend hosting your next business event here!
Nice but overpriced. It was much better before Covid when it was a French restaurant
Everything was great at the hotel. Staff was friendly and helpful. Entertainment was good and the bar/lounge was very nice

Our recent event dinner at the hotel was absolutely outstanding. From the early planning stages to the evening of our event, every detail was executed flawlessly. The preplanning team, Chelsea and Milena were lovely to work with, especially since we had several last-minute request.The food and beverage team, Jacob, Mina and Mirna delivered a truly memorable evening. The service was polished yet warm, striking the perfect balance between professionalism, casual yet hospitable.A huge thank-you to everyone involved for making this event such a success. It was an experience that showcased not only the hotel’s culinary excellence but also the dedication and teamwork behind the scenes. I have planned several events at the Culver Hotel, and they always exceed my expectations. Thank you!

We recently hosted a professional event at the Culver Hotel and had a wonderful experience from start to finish. Chelsea and Milena were an amazing coordination team—so communicative, helpful, and easy to work with. The setup was beautiful, the food was great, and Luca, our waiter, was fantastic! Everyone was so kind and accommodating.All of our guests had nothing but positive things to say and seem really interested in utilizing the venue again for future events. Thank you again to the entire team for helping make our event such a success!

The Culver hotel is a cozy, quaint boutique hotel nestled in the heart of Culver City. The rooms are spacious and well decorated. The Resturant offered live music, great food and delicious drinks and dessert. The ambiance both indoors and outdoors is lively and fun. It is easily walkable to literally everything! A must visit!
Great location and walkable to restaurants and shops

Went for dinner.  Beautiful setting, warm lights and table/chairs… lots of glass so everything glittered! Food was excellent. Parking was very convenient. Service was professional, attentive and friendly. A realJewel.  They have live jazz Tuesday through Friday, 7-10p… going back!
Great historic hotel in downtown Culver City. Shops and restaurants nearby

Still a lovely hotel after all the years! Great location with a variety of restaurants within walking distance. We didn't rent a car for try weekend because The Culver is easy access via ride share to every where on the westside to downtown. I used to live in the arts district in Culver City for many years and I could not recommend this hotel more - excellent stay, best location!

Guest unsatisfied with cleanliness of her room. towels are dirty and they were delivered to her damp? so they smell. She said the pillows smell and the room is extremely dusty. I offered to move her again but she said she just wants a deep clean and everything to be swapped out including her pillows
laurent followed up and miryam will deep clean
Hair in bathtub & Bad Dinner service
Comp lunch or Dinner extended but has not been accepted.
